narrative:
The stress score has doubled overnight, rising from 12 to 26, shifting the regime assessment from calm to cautious despite the broader equity backdrop remaining constructive. The move is driven primarily by concentrated sector weakness rather than systemic deterioration, with energy down 6.1% over five days acting as the principal drag on the distribution. Credit spreads remain tight and VIX is mid-range at 18.4 in contango, suggesting this is a sector-rotation story rather than a broad risk-off event.

The standout divergence is the 12.7 percentage point gap between EEM up 6.6% and XLE down 6.1%, reflecting a shift in capital toward emerging market growth exposure and away from commodity-linked cyclicals that warrants close monitoring for duration and breadth. A stress score sustained above 35, or a break in HY OAS beyond 320 basis points, would signal that the current sector stress is broadening into credit and would require a reassessment of the regime.
